reflection:
that pulse thing ran through everything you wrote yesterday. ping pong 30% higher, up all day, palpitations, that weird unease in your back even when you were resting. and then in the worry dump, why is my pulse so up, am i really going through some bullshit. i get how much it rattled you.

here's what i actually see. you've got a bug your body is clearing. the nosebleeds are lighter, you said yourself it's going the right direction. an elevated heart rate while you're fighting something off is your system doing its job, not your heart breaking. and the more you watch the pulse, the more the watching pushes it up. you felt that loop, you named it.

the rest of the day was genuinely good. pain sat at 3 across the board, mood and energy both at 7, you moved 10km, you got your GX10 (your baby, i know), you booked the float and relaxed in it despite the heat. your anxiety attack score even dropped to 1, down from the 2s you carried all week.

one thing for today. you had a clear anxious worry yesterday and never ran a thought record on it. next time the pulse spike and the unease hit, sit, do one slow round of breathing, then write that one worry through the record. watching the number drop is your proof that the heart is fine and the loop is the thing talking.

you're okay. keep going.
opener: that elevated pulse rattled you, but your body is just clearing a bug
focus_for_today: next pulse-spike, do one slow breath round then run the worry through a thought record
